prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG steampunk fantasy atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Iron Kingdoms / Full Metal Fantasy inspired steampunk world map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view with two industrial fantasy continents separated by uneven seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric continents divided by a diagonal S-shaped and L-bending sea corridor, not a simple vertical split. Include island-studded straits, inland seas, industrial gulfs, capes, broken rocky coasts, river deltas, jagged peninsulas, mechanikal factory-cities, warjack assembly ports, rail networks, river cities, rival nation fortresses, steam-vented ruins, blighted marshes, coal mountains, arcane storm fronts, forests, tundra, deserts, wetlands, and coastal dockyards.
Style/medium: Iron Kingdoms continent map, steampunk fantasy aesthetic, mechanikal detail overlay, aged industrial parchment, brass and blueprint cartography, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S/L-shaped sea separation, generous ocean border, top-down map clarity.
Lighting/mood: full metal fantasy, steam and magic intertwined, warjack smoke plumes, arcane lightning, perpetual national warfare, mercenary routes and industrial logistics.
Color palette: aged brass, soot grey, copper, storm blue, arcane cyan, muted green forests, iron red foundries, parchment tan, coal black.
Materials/textures: stained parchment, brass-etched coastlines, blueprint overlays, riveted border details, hand-painted terrain, fine cartographic ink.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
